research_goal: Understand how five V5 Hong Kong retail-banking personas currently manage investment portfolios, which Aladdin-based analytics functions would materially help them in real decisions, why those functions matter, and how each should be embedded into retail customer journeys without turning the experience into generic product selling.
product_context: The bank has already decided to launch a free Portfolio Health Check for retail customers using Aladdin-type analytics. This study is about practical usage design, feature usefulness, explanation boundaries, trust boundaries, and service embedding into retail banking workflows.
synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic-user interview for AI pre-validation only; not human market evidence.

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 概念驗證模式先建立最近一次真實投資組合管理行為，未有訪談證據前不應引入概念或假設痛點；首題聚焦最近一次檢視投資組合的具體經驗，以開啟後續行為與workaround重建。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 你最近一次認真睇自己成個投資組合係幾時，當時發生咩事？
open_questions: 最近一次真實投資組合檢視事件係乜
觸發佢去睇組合嘅原因係乜
當時實際睇咗邊啲持倉、畫面或資料
而家用開咩方法管理同整理投資組合
之後幾時先適合引入 Portfolio Health Check 概念

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 已取得一個最近事件，下一步先重建當時實際用咩工具同畫面，補足 current_workaround，避免太早跳去概念反應。問題錨定佢提過的『喺車度用手機逐個戶口望返』。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 你嗰陣喺車度係點樣逐個戶口望返嘅？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者最近一次認真檢視整體投資相關資產，是在今年年初交完學校雜費後，因卡數、保險和日常開支同時帶來壓力。
參與者所謂的『投資組合』目前主要包括 MPF、少量銀行基金和現金儲備，未必有正式的整體投組管理流程。
參與者當時在車上用手機逐個戶口查看，目的是判斷哪些部分要更保守。

open_questions: 當時實際打開了哪些 app、戶口或資料
她如何把 MPF、基金和現金放在一起判斷
她目前是否依賴分散的戶口畫面而非整體視圖
她所說的『要保守啲』最後有沒有引發具體行動
之後何時適合引入 Portfolio Health Check 概念

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 已經有最近事件同當時做法，下一步要釐清那次檢視後有沒有實際行動或刻意不行動，補足真實 decision/action context，之後再引入概念會更穩。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 你望完嗰次之後，最後有冇郁過任何一部分？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者當時先看銀行 app 的活期和信用卡未過數，先建立手上現金與支出壓力的概念。
參與者之後再看 MPF 和基金頁面，但不是為了研究市場，而是看分布有沒有太偏。
參與者在資金壓力情境下，也會考慮哪部分資產若突然要用錢時不想動用。
參與者目前用分開的銀行、MPF 與基金頁面自行拼湊整體判斷，而非透過正式整合式投資組合工具。

open_questions: 那次檢視後有沒有實際調整持倉、轉保守或只是維持不變
如果沒有行動，真正卡住她的是什麼
何時適合引入 Portfolio Health Check 概念
她對整合式組合檢視會先直覺覺得有冇用
她對銀行整合外部資產或更多資料的接受邊界在哪
什麼情況下這類功能會成為她的固定習慣

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 最近事件、當時做法和實際後續行動已建立，適合先用貼近她剛才情境的最小化方式引入概念，收集第一反應，再逐步探信任、嵌入時機和持續使用。避免一次塞入過多功能描述。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 如果銀行 app 有個免費嘅 Portfolio Health Check 幫你一眼睇返 MPF、基金同現金，你第一下會唔會打開嚟睇？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者在那次檢視後有做出小幅調整，把較多錢留在容易提取的地方。
參與者當時沒有新增投資，因為優先考慮的是避免一遇到開支就失去秩序，而不是追求回報。
參與者在投資組合管理上的核心取向較偏向流動性與支出緩衝，而非回報最大化。

open_questions: 她對 Portfolio Health Check 的第一反應是有用、無感還是抗拒
她若想打開，最想先看到的是整體分布、可動用資金還是其他資訊
什麼表達方式會令她覺得簡單而非太技術化
她對銀行整合這些資料的信任和資料邊界在哪
這功能應該出現在主頁、提醒、月結前還是開支壓力時刻
什麼情況下她會用一次後繼續用

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 參與者已對概念表達初步正面但有條件的反應，並主動提到不想額外設定；下一步直接探她的接受邊界，能同時補 trust_boundary，亦為之後的嵌入方式與持續使用建立條件。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 咩情況會令你一見到呢個功能就唔想再開？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者對在銀行 app 內直接看到 Portfolio Health Check 有初步興趣。
參與者傾向把這類功能當作快速概覽，而不是深入研究工具。
參與者是否願意使用，取決於資訊是否講得清楚，以及是否一開始就要求額外設定。

open_questions: 她不能接受的是額外設定、資料權限、推銷感，還是資訊太複雜
什麼呈現方式會令她信任這是中立提醒而非銷售
如果看到風險或分布提示，她實際下一步會做什麼
這功能應該出現在登入後主頁、資產頁，還是特定時刻提醒
什麼情況下她會從一次性查看變成定期使用

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 已拿到清楚的信任邊界，下一步要測試她看到具體輸出後會否採取行動，先補 action_followthrough，再用她回答帶去 repeat use 或 service embedding。問題錨定她自己提到的『現金夠唔夠、分布偏唔偏』。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 如果佢清楚話你而家現金偏少或者分布太偏，你通常下一步會點做？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者若一開始就被要求授權銀行本身已經持有的資料，會降低使用意願。
參與者若未看到任何內容就被要求預約顧問、留電話或做風險評估，多數不會再開功能。
參與者不接受空泛的『健康分數』，更重視與自己當下決策直接相關的訊息，例如現金是否足夠、分布是否偏。
參與者會把過早索取資料或導向顧問的流程解讀為產品推銷，而非中立工具。

open_questions: 看到具體風險提示後她實際會做的第一步是調現金、停手不買、再查資料，還是找人問
什麼結果會令她願意持續回來看，而不只是試一次
這功能最適合放在什麼時刻或頁面出現

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 已知道她會先自行核對未來開支再決定，下一步要確認這功能應嵌入哪個真實時刻才有用，直接補 service_embedding，並為之後判斷是否會持續使用建立場景基礎。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 呢個功能喺咩時候出現，先會令你覺得真係用得着？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者即使看到工具提示，也不會立即跟隨建議去買入或轉倉。
參與者看到『現金偏少』或『分布太偏』後，會先自行檢查未來一兩個月的固定開支，確認是否真的現金緊張。
參與者較可能採取的行動是先調回較易提取的資金，或暫停新的投入，而不是即時做投資交易。
參與者需要這類工具支持『先看清楚再決定』的流程，而不是直接給交易導向建議。

open_questions: 這功能最適合在交大額開支前、月內現金壓力時、還是定期檢視時出現
什麼條件會令她由偶爾打開變成持續使用

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 所需覆蓋只差 repeat_use_condition。參與者已說明有用時刻與入口位置，下一步直接測試什麼條件會令她持續回來用，而不是只試一次。
facilitator_decisions.message_to_persona: 要去到咩程度，你先會變成每個月都順手開嚟睇一次？

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者認為功能若在要交較大開支前出現會有用。
參與者認為功能若在月尾想看清下個月是否太緊時出現會有用。
參與者希望功能不要藏得太深，最好在已經打開銀行 app 查看戶口時順手見到。

open_questions: 什麼具體輸出或提醒會令她養成每月查看一次的習慣
她會把這功能當成固定檢查，還是只在開支壓力時才偶爾打開

facilitator_decisions.decision_rationale: 所需 coverage 已完成，且已取得最近行為、現有 workaround、概念反應、信任邊界、見到提示後的實際處理方式、嵌入時刻及持續使用條件。再追問大概率只會重複。

evidence_updates.claim: 參與者只有在功能連續幾次準確反映她當下情況時，才會養成每月查看一次的習慣。
能促成重複使用的關鍵訊號包括：提醒到現金偏緊，或指出某一部分配置過重，而且之後回看證明有用。
參與者對持續使用的前提是流程夠快、不用重新設定，並且可在十幾秒內看完重點。
參與者現時以支出壓力和流動性管理作為主軸來看投資相關資產，而不是以回報優化作為首要目標。
對這位參與者而言，Portfolio Health Check 的價值主要在於快速、非推銷式地整合現金與投資分布，支援開支前或月尾的自我檢查。

root_cause_hypotheses.hypothesis: 參與者對投資組合工具的核心需求不是更進取的投資建議，而是幫她在開支壓力下快速判斷流動性是否足夠、配置是否過偏。
alternative_explanations: 她目前持倉本來較簡單，所以只需要基礎概覽而非深入分析
她當時主要受短期現金流壓力影響，不代表平時都以流動性為先
root_cause_hypotheses.validation_gap: 只得一個近期事件，未驗證在沒有開支壓力的月份她是否仍以同樣方式管理。

root_cause_hypotheses.hypothesis: 若功能在銀行既有資料上直接生成清楚的重點概覽，並避免額外設定和推銷感，參與者較可能信任並重複使用。
alternative_explanations: 她可能只是普遍抗拒任何新功能的 onboarding，而不只針對這類投資工具
重複使用也可能主要取決於她是否剛好有大額開支周期，而不只是介面與流程
root_cause_hypotheses.validation_gap: 未有真實使用行為，只得條件式反應；仍需真人研究驗證實際留存。

root_cause_hypotheses.hypothesis: 這位參與者較適合在『已經打開銀行 app 睇戶口』及『大額開支前或月尾』等既有理財時刻接觸功能，而不是獨立主動探索。
alternative_explanations: 她可能只是因為目前時間有限，所以偏好被動入口
若未來資產複雜度提高，她也可能願意主動進入獨立投資頁面
root_cause_hypotheses.validation_gap: 未比較其他觸發時刻或通知形式，仍需更多案例驗證最佳嵌入點。

open_questions: 在沒有近期大額開支壓力時，她會否仍定期檢查整體配置
她對更進一步的分析輸出，例如情景分析或貨幣風險，會覺得有用還是過量
哪些內容應留在自助介面，哪些更適合只作 RM 輔助

exchanges.facilitator_question: 你最近一次認真睇自己成個投資組合係幾時，當時發生咩事？
exchanges.persona_response: 如果講成個投資組合，其實我冇好正式嗰種，主要都係 MPF、少少銀行基金，同埋現金儲備。最近一次認真逐樣睇，應該係今年年初交完學校雜費嗰陣，啲卡數、保險同日常開支一齊夾住，我喺車度用手機逐個戶口望返，睇下邊啲要保守啲。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 你嗰陣喺車度係點樣逐個戶口望返嘅？
exchanges.persona_response: 其實好普通，我先開銀行 app 睇活期同信用卡未過數，心入面有個數先。之後再睇 MPF 同基金嗰啲頁面，唔係研究市場，主要係望下分布有冇太偏，同埋如果突然要用錢，邊部分係唔想郁住。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 你望完嗰次之後，最後有冇郁過任何一部分？
exchanges.persona_response: 有，但唔算大動作。我記得之後係將少少錢留返多啲喺容易拎到嘅地方，冇再加任何新嘢，因為當時個感覺唔係想追回報，係想冇咁易一撞到開支就亂。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 如果銀行 app 有個免費嘅 Portfolio Health Check 幫你一眼睇返 MPF、基金同現金，你第一下會唔會打開嚟睇？
exchanges.persona_response: 會，我第一下應該會開嚟望下，如果係銀行 app 入面直接睇到，又唔使我再逐個連接戶口。 但我只係會當快速概覽，先睇佢點樣分、講得清唔清楚，同埋有冇一開波就叫我做啲額外設定。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 咩情況會令你一見到呢個功能就唔想再開？
exchanges.persona_response: 如果一開就要我授權一堆本身銀行已經有嘅資料，或者未睇到任何內容先叫我預約顧問、留電話、做風險評估，我多數就唔會再開。仲有如果個畫面好花，講啲好空泛嘅健康分數，但冇講我實際而家現金夠唔夠、分布偏唔偏，我會覺得佢只係想推產品。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 如果佢清楚話你而家現金偏少或者分布太偏，你通常下一步會點做？
exchanges.persona_response: 我多數唔會即刻跟佢建議去買或者轉。我會先自己望返未來一兩個月有咩固定開支，確認係咪真係現金太緊，之後最多都係先調返容易拎到嘅錢，或者停一停新投入，睇清楚先。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 呢個功能喺咩時候出現，先會令你覺得真係用得着？
exchanges.persona_response: 如果係我本身啱啱要交一筆比較大嘅開支之前，或者月尾想睇清楚下個月會唔會太緊，嗰陣出現我會覺得有用。仲有就係唔好藏得太深，最好喺我已經開緊銀行 app 睇戶口嗰陣，順手就見到。

exchanges.facilitator_question: 要去到咩程度，你先會變成每個月都順手開嚟睇一次？
exchanges.persona_response: 要佢真係連續幾次都講中我當下個情況，例如提醒到我現金其實偏緊，或者見到某邊擺得太重，而我之後望返係有用，我先會養成每個月順手開一次。仲有個前提係快，唔使重新設定，打開十幾秒內就睇完。

supporting_quotes.quote: 我喺車度用手機逐個戶口望返，睇下邊啲要保守啲。
先開銀行 app 睇活期同信用卡未過數，心入面有個數先。之後再睇 MPF 同基金嗰啲頁面。
當時個感覺唔係想追回報，係想冇咁易一撞到開支就亂。
如果個畫面好花，講啲好空泛嘅健康分數...我會覺得佢只係想推產品。

recent_behavior_evidence: 最近一次整體檢視發生喺交完學校雜費後，屬於真實近期事件。
她而家係分開睇活期、信用卡、MPF 同基金頁面，再自己拼返全貌。
檢視後有實際行動，但係偏向加強流動性，同時暫停新投入，而唔係交易或調倉。

existing_workaround: 用銀行 app 先睇活期同信用卡未過數，估現金壓力。
再分開睇 MPF 同基金頁面，靠自己判斷分布有冇太偏。
遇到壓力時將更多錢留喺容易拎到嘅地方。
必要時停一停新投入，等睇清楚先。
what_must_not_change: 唔好要求重新連接本身銀行已有嘅資料。
唔好一開始就導向預約顧問、留電話或做額外設定。
結果要快睇完，十幾秒內見到重點。

accepted_data_access: 銀行 app 內直接用已有銀行資料做整合。
用清楚、貼近當下決策嘅訊息，例如現金夠唔夠、分布偏唔偏。
rejected_data_access: 為咗用功能而再授權一堆銀行本身已有嘅資料。
未睇到內容前就要留電話、預約顧問、做風險評估。
required_trust_explanation: 要先俾到實際內容，再談下一步。
唔可以只俾空泛健康分數，必須講到具體情況。
畫面唔可以太花，否則會被理解成推產品。

first_use_success: 一開即見整體概覽，唔使逐個戶口自己拼。
即時指出現金是否偏緊或配置是否過偏。
abandonment_triggers: 只有抽象分數，冇具體判斷

pricing_signal.free_trial_need: 核心功能免費已符合佢期望；訪談中冇出現額外付費動機。
payment_justification: 佢關注點係是否有用、夠快、夠中立，而唔係價錢。

continuation_reasons: 連續幾次都講中當下情況。
提醒之後回望證明真係有用。
喺本來已經睇戶口嘅流程中順手見到。
drop_off_reasons: 一開就有 RM / 產品銷售感。
內容太花、太抽象、唔夠貼地。
要主動走去深層頁面先睇到。

assumption_validation.assumption: 好多零售客其實係用碎片化、事件觸發式方式管理資產，而唔係真正 whole-portfolio 流程。
assumption_validation.rationale: 呢位受訪者明確係分開睇戶口、MPF、基金，再自己心入面拼返全貌，而且通常係被開支壓力觸發。

assumption_validation.assumption: 最重要嘅 blind spot 會因人而異，唔應預設一定係集中度或重疊。
assumption_validation.rationale: 對呢位受訪者，首要問題係現金是否偏緊、邊部分唔想郁住，同分布有冇太偏；唔係追求高階分析本身。

assumption_validation.assumption: 零售客如果用簡單語言包裝，會認出某啲 Aladdin 類能力對實際決定有幫助。
assumption_validation.rationale: 她對『一眼睇返 MPF、基金同現金』有興趣，但只接受能直接支持現金與分布判斷嘅輸出。

assumption_validation.assumption: 最高價值用例係綁定真實 customer moment，而唔係單純展示 institutional analytics。
assumption_validation.rationale: 她只喺大額開支前、月尾、或已經開 app 睇戶口時覺得最有用。

assumption_validation.assumption: 有啲能力適合直接面向客戶，有啲應該留喺 RM / assisted service。
assumption_validation.rationale: 有支持簡單自助概覽，但未直接測到哪些複雜分析應只留俾 RM。

assumption_validation.assumption: 信任、資料分享舒適度、解釋方式、行動門檻會影響留存。
assumption_validation.rationale: 她清楚講到哪些設定與推銷感會令佢唔再開，亦講到要連續幾次講中先會每月打開。

assumption_validation.assumption: 深層 insight 要靠理解行為背後 root cause，而唔只係功能喜好。
assumption_validation.rationale: 她嘅核心動機係避免一撞到開支就亂，反映需求係秩序感與流動性緩衝，不係單純偏好某功能卡片。

assumption_validation.assumption: 客戶可以講清楚呢類體驗應該做乜同唔應該做乜。
assumption_validation.rationale: 她對資料、畫面、入口、速度、銷售感都有明確邊界。

key_insights: 呢位 persona 唔係用正式投資組合思維管理資產，而係喺現金流壓力出現時，臨時將銀行存款、信用卡、MPF、基金放埋一齊睇。
Portfolio Health Check 對佢嘅價值唔係『更專業分析』，而係取代手動逐頁查看，快速回答兩個問題：現金夠唔夠，同分布有冇過偏。
最有用嘅 customer moment 係大額開支前、月尾估下個月會唔會緊，或者本身已開銀行 app 睇戶口時順手見到。
第一屏必須先交付內容。任何先設定、先授權、先銷售導流都會破壞信任。
抽象健康分數對佢冇價值；要留低佢，功能要連續幾次準確反映當下情況，而且十幾秒內睇完。
insight_report.next_experiment: 喺下一輪真人訪談或原型測試，用兩種首屏版本做對比：A 版係『現金緩衝 + 分布偏離』簡潔摘要，B 版係抽象健康分數 + 多指標 dashboard，觀察受訪者第一眼理解、信任同下一步行動是否明顯分化。
evidence_gaps: 未測到佢喺冇短期開支壓力時，會唔會仍然想用同一功能。
未測到更高階分析如壓力測試、情景分析、貨幣風險對佢係過量定有用。
未直接測到哪些內容她願意自助看，哪些寧願由 RM 解釋。
只係單一 synthetic persona，唔代表其他 V5 persona。
insight_report.synthetic_only_disclaimer: 以上只係單一 synthetic persona 嘅概念驗證前測，屬方向性假設生成，唔可以當作真人市場證據或需求規模證明。

strengths: The interview established a concrete recent event before introducing the concept, using `exchange_1` to `exchange_3` to ground later reactions in actual behavior.
Most asked questions were short, conversational Cantonese and kept a single participant-facing focus.

Coverage sequencing was generally adaptive rather than questionnaire-like: recent behavior, workaround, concept reaction, trust boundary, follow-through, embedding, and repeat-use were all covered in 8 exchanges.
The synthesis preserves important weak-evidence caveats in several places, including the synthetic-only disclaimer and multiple evidence gaps.

findings.observation: The concept introduction is somewhat solution-loaded and bundles several favorable premises: `免費`, inside the bank app, and unified view of `MPF、基金同現金`. That framing can inflate first-click interest rather than neutrally testing the concept.
findings.recommendation: Introduce the concept more neutrally with fewer built-in benefits, then probe what the participant expects to see and why before asking willingness to open it.

findings.observation: Curiosity and repeat-use were separated, but trial and real follow-through remained hypothetical. `exchange_6` asks what she would do if the tool said cash was low or allocation was skewed, which is a stated intention, not observed behavior with the concept.
findings.recommendation: Label these outputs explicitly as hypothetical concept reactions and avoid treating them as behavioral proof. In the next rerun, ask for a comparable past warning or budgeting moment and compare likely tool use against that recalled case.

findings.observation: Some synthesis claims exceed the evidence from one synthetic interview. `workflow_effect: replaces_workflow` is stronger than the transcript supports; the participant said she would use it as `快速概覽` and still self-check expenses before acting. Several assumption statuses marked `supported` are too strong for one persona and largely hypothetical concept feedback.
findings.recommendation: Downgrade these to narrower claims such as `may streamline part of the current check` or `directionally supported for this persona`, and tie each claim to whether it is observed behavior or stated reaction.

findings.observation: A few synthesis conclusions rely on adjacent evidence or generalized wording beyond exact transcript support. For example, `pricing_signal.free_trial_need` says free core functionality matches her expectation, but no direct pricing tradeoff or willingness-to-pay probe was asked; `service_embedding` and `next_experiment` are reasonable design hypotheses but not participant evidence.
findings.recommendation: Keep design implications, pricing implications, and experiment ideas clearly separated from participant evidence. Mark unsupported fields as `not tested` rather than inferring from absence of objection.

findings.observation: The stated research goal asks which Aladdin-based analytics functions would materially help real decisions and how each should be embedded, but this interview mostly validates a lightweight cash-and-allocation overview. It does not directly test specific analytics functions or compare them against actual changed actions.
findings.recommendation: In the next rerun, compare 2-3 concrete decision-facing outputs tied to an actual recalled case, and ask which one would change what she does differently versus what merely sounds nice.

findings.observation: Stopping at 8 exchanges was reasonable given the declared coverage status, but one useful missing layer remained: current workaround friction was described, yet no direct contrast asked what this concept would replace, what it would not replace, or what information she would still need before acting.
findings.recommendation: If turn budget allows one extra question, ask what she would still check elsewhere after seeing the health check and what decision it would actually save her from doing manually.

required_improvements: Make concept introduction more neutral by removing stacked favorable premises from the first concept question.
Separate observed behavior from hypothetical concept reactions in synthesis labels and conclusions.
Reduce support strength for assumption validation and workflow claims that come from one synthetic persona and hypothetical responses.
Add direct probes that compare concrete analytic outputs against actual decision changes, not just stated usefulness.
Treat pricing, RM handoff boundaries, and advanced analytics suitability as untested unless directly asked.

next_interview_focus: Ask for one comparable recent money-management moment and test whether a portfolio overview would have changed a concrete action or only saved time.
Compare two specific outputs in plain language, such as `cash buffer warning` versus `allocation concentration flag`, and ask which would matter more in that recalled case.
Probe what she would still need to verify outside the tool before acting, to isolate what the tool can and cannot replace.
Ask where self-serve should end and when she would want RM/help, since assisted-service boundaries were inferred rather than directly tested.
coverage_gap_actions: Probe actual versus hypothetical follow-through: `if this had existed in that school-fees moment, what exactly would you have done differently, if anything?`
Directly test pricing instead of inferring it from positive reaction to `free` language.
Probe advanced-function fit with one concrete example each and ask whether it is useful, confusing, or overkill for this persona.
Ask whether she would use the same overview in a month without expense pressure, since repeat-use was conditioned but not contrasted against a calm month.
prompt_adjustments: For `concept_validation`, require the first concept question to avoid stacked benefits like free, pre-integrated, and no setup unless those are the variable being tested.
Force synthesis fields to label each claim as `observed past behavior`, `stated preference`, or `hypothetical concept reaction`.
Cap assumption statuses at `partially_supported` by default for a single synthetic interview unless the evidence is directly observed and tightly matched.
Add an observer rule to challenge any synthesis claim that implies replacement, retention, pricing, or product strategy without a direct participant-facing probe.
improvement_hints.turn_budget_guidance: The current soft/hard policy was sufficient for baseline concept screening, but one or two extra turns would improve decision-value evidence. Widen slightly when the research goal requires comparing specific analytics outputs or separating self-serve from assisted-service boundaries.

quality_evaluation.synthetic_only_disclaimer: This audit evaluates a single synthetic concept-validation interview. Any product, need, POV, HMW, pricing, or feature-prioritization implication should be treated as directional only and not as market evidence.

summary.overall_assessment: The facilitator ran a reasonably natural, coverage-complete interview, but accepted several high-signal participant clues without enough depth and later allowed hypothetical concept reactions to carry too much evidentiary weight in synthesis.
summary.primary_failure_mode: Coverage was completed before the facilitator isolated what the concept would actually replace, what behavior it would change, and which claims remained hypothetical rather than observed.
summary.depth_vs_coverage_assessment: Coverage sequencing was efficient and conversational, but depth was too shallow at several key moments where one additional contrast or consequence probe would have materially improved evidence quality.

facilitator_feedback_tags.why_it_matters: Completing required slots can still leave the core decision unclear if the facilitator does not test what would actually change in behavior.
facilitator_feedback_tags.observed_pattern: The interview covered recent behavior, workaround, concept reaction, trust boundary, action follow-through, embedding, and repeat-use in 8 exchanges, then stopped once coverage was complete even though the participant had not been asked what the concept would replace or what she would still need to check elsewhere.

facilitator_feedback_tags.why_it_matters: Contrast questions separate convenience value from true decision value and prevent inflated claims about behavior change.
facilitator_feedback_tags.observed_pattern: After the participant described using the concept only as a quick overview and still checking future expenses before acting, no direct contrast probe asked what the concept would save, what it would not replace, or whether it would change a real decision versus merely shorten an existing check.

facilitator_feedback_tags.why_it_matters: When stated intentions are treated like observed behavior, synthesis becomes overconfident and product-shaping conclusions can exceed evidence.
facilitator_feedback_tags.observed_pattern: The facilitator asked hypothetical follow-through and repeat-use questions after concept introduction, but the resulting answers were later used in synthesis language that implied stronger behavioral support than the transcript warranted.

facilitator_feedback_tags.why_it_matters: Bundling multiple attractive attributes into the first concept prompt can inflate positive reactions and hide which part actually matters.
facilitator_feedback_tags.observed_pattern: The first concept question introduced a free tool, inside an existing app, with unified visibility across accounts, before first probing what outcome the participant would want from such a tool.

facilitator_feedback_tags.why_it_matters: Near-miss details often reveal the threshold at which people act, defer, or abandon a workflow.
facilitator_feedback_tags.observed_pattern: When the participant said she adjusted liquidity a bit and stopped new contributions, the facilitator did not ask what would have had to be different for her to take a bigger action or no action at all.

facilitator_feedback_tags.why_it_matters: Retention and trust drivers are more reusable when each condition is isolated rather than collected as a bundle.
facilitator_feedback_tags.observed_pattern: The participant volunteered multiple conditions for trust and reuse such as speed, no setup, concrete output, and no sales feel, but the facilitator did not separate must-have conditions from nice-to-have conditions or ask which one would break adoption first.

high_value_missed_followups.trigger_type: participant said the concept would be only a quick overview
high_value_missed_followups.participant_signal: `我只係會當快速概覽` in exchange_4.persona
high_value_missed_followups.missed_followup_question: What would you still need to check after that overview before you would trust yourself to act?
high_value_missed_followups.generic_learning: When a participant narrows a concept to a lightweight role, follow with a boundary probe to learn what the tool would not replace.

high_value_missed_followups.trigger_type: participant described a real action after a recent event
high_value_missed_followups.participant_signal: `將少少錢留返多啲喺容易拎到嘅地方，冇再加任何新嘢` in exchange_3.persona
high_value_missed_followups.missed_followup_question: What would have had to be different for you to make a bigger change, or for you to leave everything untouched?
high_value_missed_followups.generic_learning: After a recalled action, use threshold or contrast probes to understand the minimum condition for different behavior.

high_value_missed_followups.trigger_type: participant rejected vague scores and sales-like flow
high_value_missed_followups.participant_signal: `健康分數` felt empty and sales-like in exchange_5.persona
high_value_missed_followups.missed_followup_question: Which part would make you close it first: the vague explanation, the request for more steps, or the feeling that it is steering you somewhere?
high_value_missed_followups.generic_learning: When multiple abandonment reasons are volunteered together, isolate the first-break condition instead of keeping them bundled.

high_value_missed_followups.trigger_type: participant described a hypothetical next step after a tool alert
high_value_missed_followups.participant_signal: In exchange_6.persona she said she would still check upcoming expenses before acting
high_value_missed_followups.missed_followup_question: Thinking back to the event you described earlier, if this tool had existed then, what exactly would it have changed in what you did, if anything?
high_value_missed_followups.generic_learning: Convert hypothetical tool reactions back into a recalled event comparison to distinguish time-saving from true behavior change.

high_value_missed_followups.trigger_type: participant gave a repeat-use condition bundle
high_value_missed_followups.participant_signal: `講中當下個情況`, `有用`, `快`, `唔使重新設定` in exchange_8.persona
high_value_missed_followups.missed_followup_question: If only one of those were true on the first few uses, which one would matter most for you to come back?
high_value_missed_followups.generic_learning: When participants name multiple retention conditions, ask them to rank or break ties so the strongest driver is not lost.

likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.observed_surface_frame: The participant seemed to want a portfolio-health feature that summarizes accounts.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.possible_underlying_driver: The more fundamental driver may have been reducing uncertainty before acting under time or consequence pressure, with summary only serving that broader control need.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.why_the_surface_frame_is_weak: The transcript shows the participant still planned to verify expenses and preserve optionality before acting, so the summary itself may not be the core driver.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.generic_learning: Do not equate enthusiasm for summary or visibility with demand for a feature; probe what risk or uncertainty that summary helps manage.

likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.observed_surface_frame: The participant seemed mainly sensitive to trust and onboarding friction.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.possible_underlying_driver: The deeper issue may have been skepticism toward tools that ask for commitment before proving immediate relevance.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.why_the_surface_frame_is_weak: The participant objected not only to extra steps but also to getting no useful content before being asked to do more.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.generic_learning: When participants mention setup friction, test whether the real barrier is effort, premature commitment, or lack of immediate proof of value.

likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.observed_surface_frame: The participant seemed suitable for passive in-flow exposure rather than active exploration.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.possible_underlying_driver: The stronger pattern may be situational relevance: people engage when a current decision makes the information consequential, regardless of channel placement.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.why_the_surface_frame_is_weak: The transcript establishes useful moments, but not whether visibility alone would matter absent a live decision context.
likely_misclassified_driver_patterns.generic_learning: Do not treat preferred placement as a stable adoption driver until you separate context timing from navigation convenience.

evidence_handling_issues.issue: Hypothetical concept reactions were allowed to support synthesis claims with stronger language than the interview evidence justified.
evidence_handling_issues.generic_learning: Require synthesis to distinguish observed past behavior from stated future intent and from reaction to a proposed concept.

evidence_handling_issues.issue: Some conclusions implied workflow replacement even though the participant explicitly described continued manual verification before action.
evidence_handling_issues.generic_learning: Do not infer replacement from convenience value; ask what the participant would still do outside the concept before treating it as substitutive.

evidence_handling_issues.issue: Untested fields were partially filled by inference from positive or negative reactions rather than direct probes.
evidence_handling_issues.generic_learning: Mark areas like pricing, escalation boundaries, and advanced-fit questions as not tested unless a participant-facing question addressed them directly.

prompt_adjustments.text: When a participant says a concept would be only a quick check, must ask one follow-up on what it would not replace before moving on.

In concept validation, every synthesis claim must be labeled internally as observed past behavior, recalled-event comparison, or hypothetical concept reaction; hypothetical evidence cannot justify replacement or retention claims on its own.
After a participant reports a real action in a recent event, ask one threshold or contrast probe before introducing the concept unless turn budget is already constrained.
When a participant volunteers multiple adoption or abandonment conditions in one answer, prioritize isolating the strongest condition before opening a new coverage area.
First concept-introduction questions should avoid stacking multiple favorable premises unless the test is specifically about those premises.
Do not stop solely because coverage slots are complete if the interview still lacks a clear answer to whether the concept changes behavior, saves time only, or leaves the core workaround intact.

carry_forward_rules.rule: After a participant recalls a real action, ask at least one threshold, contrast, or near-miss follow-up before moving into concept reaction.
Treat participant statements about what they would do with a proposed concept as hypothetical until tied back to a specific recalled event comparison.
When multiple reasons for trust, drop-off, or reuse are volunteered together, isolate the break-point condition instead of carrying the bundle forward unchanged.
Before concluding a concept streamlines or replaces a workflow, ask what the participant would still need to check, verify, or do elsewhere.

blocked_feedback.blocked_item: The interview should have compared specific analytics outputs tied to this product context earlier.
blocked_feedback.block_reason: This is too tied to the specific product and research goal to be safely reused as facilitator-core guidance.
blocked_feedback.rewrite_as_generic: When a study depends on which outputs materially influence decisions, compare two or three concrete outputs against the same recalled event rather than validating a broad concept at a high level.

blocked_feedback.blocked_item: The facilitator should have probed whether this banking feature belongs in an account view versus a deeper investment page.
blocked_feedback.block_reason: This is too specific to the current channel and product embedding context.
blocked_feedback.rewrite_as_generic: When participants mention where they want to encounter a feature, separate situational timing from location preference before treating placement as the primary adoption driver.
